skill_gaps_addressed: The skill's 'New rcParam?' checklist says to update typing.py with a new RcKeyType Literal entry. It should clarify that this applies only when adding a *new* key — not when extending the validator for an existing key. This distinction avoids a false-positive flag when a reviewer incorrectly reports typing.py as missing for extended validators.

The skill mentions 'doc/api/next_api_changes/behavior_changes/' (with _changes suffix) in the architecture tree, but real PRs use 'doc/api/next_api_changes/behavior/' (without suffix). The skill should consistently use the actual directory name or explicitly note the distinction, to avoid reviewers citing the wrong path.

The skill has no guidance on PostScript-specific conventions for backend_ps.py. It should mention that new PS rendering methods must call 'newpath' before accumulating path segments (since gsave does not clear the current path), and should fill glyphs individually rather than accumulating them into a single path, matching the behavior of the PDF and SVG backends.

The skill's test section says to use pytest.warns for deprecated-API tests but does not mention the rc_context pattern for rcParams mutation in tests. Adding an explicit rule — 'tests that modify rcParams must use mpl.rc_context(...) or the @mpl.rc_context decorator' — would make this catch systematic rather than ad-hoc.

The skill does not address the requirement to post reviews to GitHub as formal inline comments via the MCP GitHub tools. The review was correctly identified as review.json output but never submitted to the PR. The skill should specify that after generating review.json, the reviewer agent must use mcp__github__create_pull_request_review with inline comments to actually post the review to GitHub.

The skill's API Stability checklist mentions deprecation when 'a function signature changing' but does not explicitly state that changing a property's *return type* (e.g., Colorizer.clip going from 'bool' to 'tuple[bool]', Colorizer.vmin from 'float | None' to 'tuple[float | None, ...]') is equally a breaking change requiring the same deprecation protocol. The reviewer caught get_clim() but missed clip, vmin, and vmax which follow the identical pattern. Add explicit guidance: 'Any change to the return type of a public property or method is a breaking API change.'

The skill lists 'New feature? Add a .rst file under doc/release/next_whats_new/' in its Documentation checklist but does not highlight this as a hard blocker or cross-reference it against the API Stability checklist. The reviewer correctly identified the breaking API change in get_clim() but never flagged the absence of any doc/release/next_whats_new/ RST for the new BivarColorbar class, Figure.bivar_colorbar(), and pyplot.bivar_colorbar(). Strengthen the checklist: 'REQUIRED for any new public class/function: verify a .rst exists in doc/release/next_whats_new/ AND that the docstring contains .. versionadded:: 3.N'.

The skill lists 'doc/api/next_api_changes/' as a place for API-change RST notes but does not make clear that a missing RST entry is itself a review blocker even if the deprecation code is present. The reviewer correctly described the _api.warn_deprecated fix for get_clim() but never noted that a corresponding RST file must also be added. Add to the checklist: 'Any deprecated or removed API must have both: (a) _api.warn_deprecated/deprecated in code AND (b) a .rst file in doc/api/next_api_changes/deprecations/.'

The skill section on 'All files changed in PR' has no explicit instruction to list and confirm coverage of every changed file before concluding the review. The reviewer examined 5 of 30 changed files and missed 245 additions to test_axes.py, 37+/15- changes in pyplot.py, 48+/19- in axes/_axes.py, and changes to image.py, collections.py, and backend_bases.py. Add to the process: 'Before writing your review, list all changed files and explicitly confirm which were examined. Flag any file you did not review.'

The skill does not mention that when pyplot.py changes mirror Axes or Figure method signatures, the reviewer should verify the inline type annotations in pyplot.py match the stub file. The pyplot.py changes to bivar_colorbar() and imshow() type hints were not checked for consistency with figure.pyi and axes/_axes.pyi.
